T1.explanationTemplate: Personal use with low risk; a quick sense-check is enough.
checklist: Read for obvious mismatches or odd labels.

T2.explanationTemplate: Personal use with low risk; no formal verification is needed.
checklist: Skim for anything obviously omitted or distorted.

T3.explanationTemplate: Personal drafting with low risk; use after a quick read-through.
checklist: Read once for tone, clarity, and obvious mistakes.

T4.explanationTemplate: Low-risk personal code use still benefits from a quick check.
checklist: Read the code and confirm it broadly matches your intent.

T5.explanationTemplate: Personal translation with low risk needs only a light review.
checklist: Check that the meaning roughly matches what you intended.

T6.explanationTemplate: Low-risk transformation can be used after a quick visual check.
checklist: Check the output format looks correct.

T7.explanationTemplate: Exploratory use is fine here, but do not treat it as established fact.
checklist: Use as a draft for thinking, not as verified truth.

T8.explanationTemplate: Low-risk evaluation can be used informally with a quick check.
checklist: Check the output does not obviously conflict with your judgment.

T9.explanationTemplate: Even for personal use, numerical work deserves a brief check.
checklist: Check the inputs and sense-check the result.

T10.explanationTemplate: Policy interpretation is rarely safe to trust without review.
checklist: Treat this as provisional until checked against the rule text.

T1.explanationTemplate: Internal draft use with low stakes calls for a quick review.
checklist: Check a few sample labels manually.
Flag anything ambiguous before sharing.

T2.explanationTemplate: Internal use calls for a light source check before sharing.
checklist: Check the main points against the source.
Look for missing context or distortions.

T3.explanationTemplate: Internal drafting should be reviewed before circulation.
checklist: Read for clarity and tone.
Remove any unsupported claims before sharing.

T4.explanationTemplate: Internal code drafts need a brief review before use.
checklist: Read the code for obvious errors.
Check it matches the intended task.

T5.explanationTemplate: Translation for internal use needs a quick meaning check.
checklist: Check key terms and intended tone.
Confirm no major meaning has shifted.

T6.explanationTemplate: Transformation for internal use should be spot-checked.
checklist: Check a few rows or records.
Confirm the new format is correct.

T7.explanationTemplate: Internal planning or research needs a light plausibility review.
checklist: Check the main claims look credible.
Mark anything uncertain or speculative.

T8.explanationTemplate: Internal evaluation should be checked for obvious bias or inconsistency.
checklist: Review a few outputs manually.
Check whether the scoring logic seems consistent.

T9.explanationTemplate: Numerical outputs should get a quick check before internal use.
checklist: Check the inputs.
Sense-check the magnitude of the result.

T10.explanationTemplate: Internal policy interpretations still need checking against the actual rule.
checklist: Read the relevant rule text.
Check the output matches the stated criteria.

T1.explanationTemplate: This supports decisions, so verify labels against clear criteria.
checklist: Check a sample against the classification rules.
Review borderline cases.
Mark uncertain items for manual review.

T2.explanationTemplate: This output informs decisions and should be checked against source material.
checklist: Read the source and confirm key points.
Check nothing important was omitted.
Flag any ambiguity before use.

T3.explanationTemplate: Use in decisions requires checking the draft for unsupported content.
checklist: Check factual claims against source material.
Remove unsupported assertions.
Review whether the wording could mislead.

T4.explanationTemplate: Code that supports decisions should be tested before reliance.
checklist: Run or validate the code if possible.
Check key logic branches.
Confirm outputs match expectations.

T5.explanationTemplate: Translation used in decisions needs a closer meaning check.
checklist: Check critical terms carefully.
Confirm nuances were preserved.
Review any legally or operationally important wording.

T6.explanationTemplate: Transformation that informs decisions should be checked against the source format.
checklist: Check the transformed output against the original.
Spot-check key fields.
Confirm nothing was lost or altered.

T7.explanationTemplate: Research or planning used in decisions needs grounded verification.
checklist: Verify the key claims with trusted sources.
Check whether alternatives were overlooked.
Flag assumptions that need human review.

T8.explanationTemplate: Evaluation used in decisions needs consistency and sample checking.
checklist: Review examples across the score range.
Check the scoring is applied consistently.
Escalate uncertain cases for manual review.

T9.explanationTemplate: Decision-relevant numerical analysis should be independently checked.
checklist: Recheck the inputs and units.
Redo the core calculation independently.
Confirm the result is plausible before use.

T10.explanationTemplate: Rule or policy application used in decisions requires direct checking.
checklist: Compare the output to the actual rule text.
Check definitions and exceptions.
Have unclear cases reviewed by a human.

T1.explanationTemplate: This is consequential enough to require independent review.
checklist: Check outputs against the classification criteria.
Review edge and borderline cases.
Have another person review before use.
Do not rely on uncertain labels without escalation.

T2.explanationTemplate: Externally used or consequential summaries need independent checking.
checklist: Check all key points against the source.
Confirm nothing material was omitted or distorted.
Have someone else review before use.
Do not share if any ambiguity remains.

T3.explanationTemplate: External or operational drafting needs stronger review before use.
checklist: Check all factual claims.
Review tone and audience risk.
Have another person approve before sharing.
Remove anything you cannot verify.

T4.explanationTemplate: Consequential code output requires independent testing or review.
checklist: Run the code or validate syntax.
Test the main path and one edge case.
Have someone else review the logic.
Do not use it untested.

T5.explanationTemplate: Translation with external or operational use needs independent review.
checklist: Check critical wording carefully.
Confirm meaning and register are preserved.
Have another person review important passages.
Escalate if any term is ambiguous.

T6.explanationTemplate: Operational transformations should be checked before use.
checklist: Compare output against original records.
Spot-check multiple examples.
Have someone else review if the output drives action.
Do not proceed if fields appear altered.

T7.explanationTemplate: Consequential research or planning needs source-based validation and review.
checklist: Verify key claims with trusted sources.
Check for missed assumptions or alternatives.
Have someone else review before acting on it.
Do not treat unverified claims as facts.

T8.explanationTemplate: Consequential scoring or evaluation needs independent review.
checklist: Review examples manually.
Check consistency across cases.
Have another reviewer approve use.
Escalate outputs affecting people or outcomes.

T9.explanationTemplate: Numerical outputs at this level require independent review before use.
checklist: Redo the main calculation independently.
Check units, assumptions, and inputs.
Have another person review the result.
Do not act until discrepancies are resolved.

T10.explanationTemplate: Policy interpretation at this level requires direct checking and review.
checklist: Compare against the actual rule text.
Check definitions, exceptions, and thresholds.
Have a human reviewer approve the interpretation.
Do not act on unresolved ambiguity.

T1.explanationTemplate: This use case needs formal controls, not just ad hoc review.
checklist: Check against documented criteria.
Review a sample and edge cases.
Require accountable sign-off.
Document any uncertainty or exceptions.

T2.explanationTemplate: High-stakes summaries need formal validation before use.
checklist: Check all material points against the source.
Document omissions or ambiguities.
Require approval from an accountable reviewer.
Do not use without traceable validation.

T3.explanationTemplate: High-stakes drafting requires formal review and approval.
checklist: Verify all factual claims.
Review wording for legal or reputational risk.
Require formal sign-off.
Keep a record of what was checked.

T4.explanationTemplate: High-stakes code use requires formal validation and approval.
checklist: Test the code before use.
Review logic and failure modes.
Require accountable approval.
Keep a record of checks performed.
Check critical wording carefully.
Review sensitive or regulated terms.
Require approval from an accountable reviewer.
Document any unresolved nuance.

T6.explanationTemplate: High-stakes data transformations need formal checking.
checklist: Validate transformed output against source data.
Check critical fields systematically.
Require accountable sign-off.
Keep a record of the checks.

T7.explanationTemplate: High-stakes research or planning needs formal validation.
checklist: Verify key claims with trusted sources.
Document assumptions and limitations.
Require formal review before acting.
Do not use unsupported claims.

T8.explanationTemplate: High-stakes evaluation needs formal controls and review.
checklist: Check outputs against defined criteria.
Review representative cases and edge cases.
Require accountable approval.
Document any exceptions or uncertainty.

T9.explanationTemplate: High-stakes numerical analysis requires formal checking and sign-off.
checklist: Redo the analysis independently.
Check inputs, units, and assumptions.
Require accountable review.
Document the validation performed.

T10.explanationTemplate: High-stakes policy interpretation needs formal human review.
checklist: Compare against the source rule text.
Check definitions, exceptions, and thresholds.
Require accountable approval.
Document the basis for the interpretation.
